calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2024 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-25-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. STEPHEN SIBBACH, Esq. of Law Office of Stephen E. Sibbach (tel: 805-464-6921) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Wells Fargo's motion for summary judgment is granted.

Wells Fargo alleges that defendant Michael Skersick used a WF credit card and failed to remit payments after July 2022, leaving a balance of almost $20,000. Wells Fargo now brings this motion for summary judgment.
Summary judgment is appropriate where there is no triable issue of material fact and the moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. (CCP 437(c).) To meet its burden on summary judgment, a moving plaintiff must show that there is no defense to a cause of action if that party has proved each element of the cause of action entitling the party to judgment on the cause of action. (CCP 437(c)(p)(1).) Once the plaintiff has met that burden, the burden shifts to the defendant to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists as to the cause of action or a defense thereto. (Id.) The defendant shall not rely upon the allegations or denials of its pleadings to show that a triable issue of material fact exists but, instead, shall set forth the specific facts in its opposition. (Id.) A triable issue of a material fact exists "if, and only if, the evidence would allow a reasonable trier of fact to find the underlying fact in favor of the party opposing the motion in accordance with the applicable standard of proof." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 845.)
Wells Fargo has met its burden to show that it has proved each element of each cause of action. Skersick applied for and was issued a credit card, and accepted the terms of the customer agreement by using the credit card. (Declaration of Plaintiff's Qualified Witness, Exhibits 1-2.) Skersick failed to remit payment to Wells Fargo for his debt after July 2022. (Id.)

The burden shifts to Skersick to show a triable issue of one or more material facts or a defense to a cause of action. In opposition, Skersick only provides his own declaration to attempt to create a triable issue of material fact. Skersick provides no supporting facts or evidence for his contention that he did not agree to pay the listed charges. Skersick has provided no proof that he contacted Wells Fargo to advise them that he did not agree to the listed charges or the interest rate, nor does he provide any evidence that he did not agree to the contract. The declaration as submitted is insufficient to create a triable issue of material fact. (Featherstone v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (2017) 10 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1159, citing King v. United Parcel Service, Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 426, 433.)

Order signed. Judgment signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
